sglang.title: [RadixTree] Reduce Stack Push/Pop Overhead for Leaf Nodes, Improve radix_tree Leaf Collection Performance
sglang.author: CLFutureX
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Background
Currently, when collecting leaf nodes, the process first traverses child nodes and pushes them onto the stack. Only after popping the nodes from the stack does it check if they are leaf nodes.
Optimization
During the traversal of child nodes, immediately check if a node is a leaf node:
If

vllm.title: [Model][Qwen3VL] Slighly speedup `fast_pos_embed_interpolate`
vllm.author: lgeiger
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This slightly reduces the overhead of fast_pos_embed_interpolate by using an in-place multiplication. Followup to #26647
In a simple benchmark this speedups the function by ~20% for bfloat16 and ~30% for float32. Nothing major, but it's still a nice win.

vllm.title: [NIXL] Generalize block-first backend layouts (FlashInfer-like)
vllm.author: NickLucche
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR generalizes the handling of attention backends that make use of a "swapped" layout wrt the historically default one (ie FlashAttn).
To clarify:
# "Default" layout (FlashAttn)
2, num_blocks, N, H, D
# "Swapped" layout (FlashInfer)
num_blocks, 2, N, H, D

Mind that in the latter case we're abl

vllm.author: izhuhaoran
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Inspired by TensorRT-LLM. This PR is a follow PR about #27018 , and fuses QNorm, KNorm, and RoPE into a single CUDA kernel for the Qwen3 model, improving inference performance. We convert this fusion into a custom torch.compile pass, users can enable it by:
 --compilation-config='{"use_induc

vllm.title: [ROCm][Quantization] extend AMD Quark to support mixed-precision quantized model
vllm.author: xuebwang-amd
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
This PR aims to support layerwise mixed-precision quantization model inference, extending from quantized models in single scheme such as MXFP4, FP8 (aka PTQ models).
Here, the layerwise mixed-precision configuration for a given model is searched and then quantized by amd-quark. Specifically,

vllm.title: [Quantization] fix attention quantization of gpt_oss model
vllm.author: xuebwang-amd
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
There are mainly two aiming purposes of this PR:

Enable attention quantization (FP8, MXFP4, UnquantizedLinearMethod, etc) by passing quant_config to OAIAttention and associated calling classes.
Change the packed_modules_mapping from {"qkv": ["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj"]} to {"qkv_proj": ["

vllm.author: zhewenl
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose

This PR will help #28287 (comment) by changing the ground truth for attention backend from xformers to pytorch SDPA
Fixes some incompatibilities on AMD:
a. The ROCm paged attention kernel expects 32-bit int tensors, but the test passes 64-bit torch.long tensors.
b. ROCm paged attention kern

vllm.title: Prefer FlashAttention MLA as default over FlashMLA
vllm.author: MatthewBonanni
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Based on benchmarking in #26835, FlashAttention MLA is faster than FlashMLA across head counts, batch sizes, and query lengths on Hopper. This PR sets it as the preferred backend.
Based on #24794, merge that first
Note: Results shown below apply the fix of #27368, FlashMLA performance is wor

vllm.title: [Kernel] Optimize rms_norm kernel
vllm.author: xyang16
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Optimize rms_norm kernel:

Do vectorized load and store using vec_n_t<scalar_t, VEC_SIZE>.
Since input, weight, output tensors are the same dtype, and all of their last dimension are hidden_size, we can get the largest safe vec_size by calculating the number that can divide hidden_size and f

vllm.title: [Perf] Use np.ndarray instead of list[list[int]] to reduce GC overhead
vllm.author: Jialin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
LogprobsLists would introduced 3 nested list[list[int]] which would invoke severe GC costs for large batch size use cases.
In this PR, we're simply changing the nested list to np.ndarray, and ideally the interface should be mostly identical compared to the original one.

This PR simplifies the Qwen3VL get_mrope_input_positions computation by using np.indices to make the code more readable.
In a profile I also noticed that the torch CPUs ops, especially torch.tensor(list[int]) are slower than their numpy equivalents so this PR also changes the computation to

vllm.title: [Core] Encoder separation for Encode-Prefill-Decode Disaggregation
vllm.author: fake0fan
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Disaggregated Encoder
A disaggregated encoder runs the vision-encoder stage of a multimodal LLM in a process that is separate from the prefill / decoder stage. Deploying these two stages in independent vLLM instances brings three practical benefits:

Independent, fine-grained scaling
Lower time-to-f

vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
This PR enables Kwai-Keye/Keye-VL-8B-Preview and Kwai-Keye/Keye-VL-1_5-8B on XPU by adding a fallback path in the apply_rotary_pos_emb_flashattn function. Otherwise, the code would throw an error that apply_rotary_emb is not available.
Test Plan
python3 examples/offline_inference/vision_lang

vllm.title: [Benchmark] Add retry support to fix workload bias in multi-turn benchmark
vllm.author: ai-jz
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Fix workload bias in multi-turn benchmark where timed-out clients exit conversations immediately, reducing actual workload to model servers with lower capacity.
E.g., when workload is high (# of clients=64 or 128 with long-context requests), vLLM baseline has more timeouts than vLLM + LMCach

vllm.title: [Core] Cache `vllm_is_batch_invariant`
vllm.author: lgeiger
vllm.bodyExcerpt: vllm_is_batch_invariant is called many times during inference which is noticeable in a profile. We should cache it such that we don't need to repeatedly call os.getenv().
